checklist.claim: engine-surface change carries a bump: changeset
CLI-contract / digest / coupled-site claims (verified by code-reviewer + requesting-code-review against argparse surface and shipped code)

phase3_findings.description: main() has no top-level catch-all; queries-always-exit-0 holds by construction not enforcement
emit-body foreign-nonce refusal not directly driven
record shape coupled across 3 sites (disclosed accepted trade-off)
creation record referential integrity validated weaker than sibling revision (epoch_round has no reader today)
attestation=none conflates never-attempted vs epoch-bound-unattested (skill layer mitigates)

fix_decisions.claim_text: main() lacks a top-level exception guard
fix_decisions.evidence: Contract holds by construction and is covered by the malformed-state matrix + per-command guards; agent could not construct a crashing input. A blanket except-Exception -> fail-closed-token in a state owner would launder a genuine query-path logic bug into a silent token, against the repo's 'never launder unknown onto a value' philosophy; the loud crash is more diagnosable and the skill's no-parseable-token fallback already degrades it safely.

fix_decisions.claim_text: record shape lives in 3 coupled sites; use dataclasses
fix_decisions.evidence: Disclosed accepted trade-off documented in-code with a stated revisit trigger; save_state re-validation bounds it to a loud persist-time failure (not fail-open). Prescription reverses a documented design decision.

fix_decisions.claim_text: creation.epoch_round not cross-ref validated like revision
fix_decisions.evidence: epoch_round/epoch_arm have no reader today (disclosed in-code); validating a reader-less field adds surface without closing a reachable fail-direction. Latent, not a live defect.

Graded cosmetic and already mitigated in the skill layer (mandated 'attestation errored post-fetch' override). Changing the _SUMMARY_FIELDS token vocabulary risks coupled-site churn (lockstep tests + skill prose + run.sh pins) disproportionate to a narrow already-covered window.

skipped_checks.reason: working tree dirty (pre-commit run); re-ran clean on committed HEAD -> PASS, 0 skipped
